title: Julie's Korean Fried Chicken
ingredients: 6 skinless, boneless chicken breasts or 8 thighs
instructions: Cut each chicken breast into 2 strips and place chicken in a plastic bag.
Make the marinade by mixing gochujang, salt, minced garlic cloves, and grated ginger.
Add approximately 2 cups water to the marinade and pour over the chicken.
Place the chicken in the refrigerator for 4-10 hours.
Make the sauce by combining gochujang, honey, brown sugar, soy sauce, minced garlic, grated fresh ginger, vegetable oil, and toasted sesame oil in a saucepan.
Heat the sauce mixture to boiling, stirring frequently, and boil while stirring for a couple of minutes. Turn off heat.
Mix potato starch, salt, pepper, and ground ginger together in a pie plate.
Remove chicken from refrigerator and drain the marinade.
Place chicken in the potato starch mixture and press the starch firmly onto the chicken until fully covered.
Place chicken in hot oil in batches as needed and cook for 5-6 minutes.
Remove chicken and place on paper towels to drain.
Let chicken cool for approximately 10-15 minutes.
Refry chicken for approximately 3 minutes until thoroughly crispy.
Place chicken in a large bowl and pour gochujang sauce over it, tossing to mix thoroughly.
Place on a platter and sprinkle toasted sesame seeds on top.
Serve with rice, kimchi, pickled cucumbers, and pickled radishes.
notes: Use either chicken breasts or thighs—8 thighs if using thighs instead of 6 breasts
The double-frying technique ensures extra crispy coating
Add red chili flakes if you prefer more heat in the dish
Marinating for 4-10 hours allows the chicken to absorb the gochujang flavors
